title: arenaria procera subsp. pubescens
intro: Arenaria procera subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy rock-campion, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Arenaria procera subsp. pubescens are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing its roots and leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
